message:
docs(conformance): document DISC-011 Int64+null Float64 promotion (br-mywg / fd90.76)

Self-review of fd90.75 surfaced 5 fp-conformance test failures.
Root-cause analysis: 2 of the 3 packet_filter_runs failures
(FP-P2D-028 dataframe_concat_axis1 and FP-P2D-433
dataframe_to_json_records) share a common discrepancy:

  When an Int64 column gains null values (typically via index
  alignment over a wider index, e.g. concat axis=1), our impl
  promotes the column dtype to Float64 with NaN. Pandas (since
  v0.24) has a nullable Int64 extension dtype that preserves
  the integer encoding via a separate validity mask. The
  conformance oracle uses extension Int64 where the column was
  originally int64. Downstream IO (JSON serializer) then writes
  '"a":1.0' instead of '"a":1' for integer columns that got
  promoted, surfacing as actual=Float64(1.0), expected=Int64(1)
  mismatches.

Document this as DISC-011 WILL-FIX in
crates/fp-conformance/DISCREPANCIES.md so:
  - Future readers running into 1.0 vs 1 mismatches understand
    the root cause without having to re-derive it.
  - The 2 affected packet families have an explicit documented
    home rather than appearing as orphan flakes.
  - The architectural fix (implementing nullable extension Int64
    across fp-columnar / fp-frame / fp-io) is tracked as a
    future epic separate from the fd90 SQL backend work.

Pure documentation. Build passes; this only adds a markdown
section. The 5 conformance test failures remain (they're
WILL-FIX); my recent fd90.x commits don't introduce them and
the README at line 869 (fd90.75) now accurately describes the
known-failure set.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"frankenpandas-mxho","title":"fp-conformance: fuzz_excel_io_bytes assert_excel_roundtrip suffers same index-column leak as 989b","description":"Observed: tests::fuzz_excel_io_bytes_accepts_valid_seed_fixture fails with FpIoError::Io 'excel round-trip drifted after parse/write/reparse'.

Root: assert_excel_roundtrip (crates/fp-conformance/src/lib.rs:4946) writes via write_excel_bytes (default index=true emits index column) but reads via read_excel_bytes(default, no index_col), so the original index leaks back as a leading data column. frame.equals(reparsed) returns false.

Fix: parallel to 989b — write with ExcelWriteOptions { index: false, ..default } so writer output matches default reader input. Public write_excel_bytes default unchanged.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T06:36:44.585088486Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T06:39:08.736015614Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T06:39:08.733979574Z","close_reason":"shipped 2867eff: assert_excel_roundtrip now writes with ExcelWriteOptions{index:false}, mirroring 989b's fix. fuzz_excel_io_bytes_accepts_valid_seed_fixture passes; both fuzz_excel tests now green. Public write_excel_bytes default unchanged. Clippy clean.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-myra","title":"[MEDIUM] fuzz: add fuzz_semantic_eq_invariants target (lock pxv5 Null-bridging invariants)","description":"br-frankenpandas-pxv5 just added a Null(_)/Null(_) bridge to Scalar::semantic_eq. The existing fuzz corpus has no target that exercises the semantic_eq lattice, which means future regressions to that bridge (or to the Float64<->Null(NaN) bridge) won't be caught by fuzz.

Proposed target: fuzz_semantic_eq.rs:
1. Read 4 byte tags: dtype_a, dtype_b, value_a, value_b. Build Scalar a and b from each (using fuzz_feather_scalar_for_dtype-style projection).
2. Call a.semantic_eq(&b) and b.semantic_eq(&a) and assert symmetric.
3. Assert reflexive: x.semantic_eq(&x) is always true (including x = Null(NaN), Null(Null), Null(NaT), NaN-Float64).
4. Assert that any pair of Null(_) variants returns true (locks the pxv5 contract).
5. Assert that Float64(NaN).semantic_eq(Null(NaN)) is true and that Float64(NaN).semantic_eq(Null(Null)) is also true (both are missing).
6. Never panics on any byte input.

New fp-conformance pub fn fuzz_semantic_eq_bytes(input: &[u8]). Locks the equivalence-relation invariants (reflexive/symmetric/null-bridge) so the next refactor of semantic_eq can't silently break oracle parity downstream.","notes":"cc-pandas triage 2026-04-22: implemented in fuzz_targets/fuzz_semantic_eq.rs + fp_conformance::fuzz_semantic_eq_bytes (lib.rs:5521). Locks reflexive/symmetric invariants and the Null(_)/Null(_) + Float64(NaN)<->Null(_) bridges per pxv5 contract. Landed in commit e142c21.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-22T08:24:20.601487209Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T10:33:37.756290727Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T10:33:37.753737628Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"frankenpandas-mywg","title":"fd90.76: DISC-011 - Int64 null promotes to Float64 (no nullable Int64 extension dtype)","description":"Self-review of fd90.75 surfaced 5 fp-conformance test failures. 3 of them (FP-P2D-028 dataframe_concat_axis1, FP-P2D-433 dataframe_to_json_records, plus the same pattern in other places) share a common root cause: when an Int64 column gets a null value (typically via index alignment introducing rows with no source data), our impl promotes the column dtype to Float64 with NaN. Pandas (since v0.24) has a nullable Int64 extension dtype that preserves the integer encoding via a separate validity mask. The conformance oracle expects the pandas nullable-Int64 representation. Document this as DISC-011 ACCEPTED with status WILL-FIX-LATER. Implementing nullable-Int64 is a major architectural change (storage, arithmetic, serialization, IO) tracked under a separate epic. This bead just captures the discrepancy for users running into 1.0 vs 1 mismatches in JSON output, concat axis=1 results, etc.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"docs","assignee":"cc-pandas-2","created_at":"2026-04-26T07:28:53.153542361Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T07:30:48.390025390Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T07:30:48.387587014Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["discrepancy","docs","fd90","review"]}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-n0sh","title":"fp-frame Series.ndim property parity","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T09:29:19.648682194Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T09:34:57.167202898Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T09:34:57.165272633Z","close_reason":"Implemented Series::ndim in commit 141b6bf, 1 test","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-n13","title":"Add Series.cut conformance test coverage","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-17T07:28:34.395866085Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-17T07:30:18.918358706Z","closed_at":"2026-04-17T07:30:18.916046036Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-n609","title":"[MEDIUM] conformance: COVERAGE.md stale (814 reported vs 1249 actual fixtures; no row-MI / str accessor listings)","description":"Applying /testing-conformance-harnesses skill's Coverage Accounting Matrix rule.

**Observed state:** crates/fp-conformance/COVERAGE.md (9.8KB, last touched 2026-04-18) reports a total of 814 fixtures. Counting the actual corpus today (find crates/fp-conformance/fixtures/packets -maxdepth 1 -name '*.json' | wc -l) returns **1249** fixtures. The doc is **53% under-reported** — 435 fixtures landed between the 2026-04-18 snapshot and today without COVERAGE.md being regenerated.

**Also missing from COVERAGE.md:**
- The Window Functions row shows 5 fixtures; today the rolling family alone has roughly 30 (mean/sum/min/max/count/std/var across Series + DataFrame + grouped + resample).
- No row for row MultiIndex (br-frankenpandas-1zzp.* slices landed 1-5 today; slice 6 conformance coverage still open as 1zzp.5).
- No row for Excel default round-trip (br-ho6t fix + detection helper).
- No row for Series str* 50+ accessors (README says 50+ but COVERAGE.md is silent).

**Violates skill requirements:**
- Rule: "Before claiming conformance, prove it" — the table claims 214 operations / 814 fixtures but reality is closer to 430 packets / 1249 fixtures. The claim is not provable against the reported numbers.
- Rule: "Score < 0.95 for MUST clauses = NOT conformant. Ship with known gaps documented, never with unknown gaps." — unknown gaps exist because the doc doesn't list what landed.
- Compliance report generator pattern (skill's Pattern 4 template) — COVERAGE.md is hand-maintained; should be auto-generated from a traversal of fixtures/packets/ + a registry of operations under test.

**Fix shape:**
1. Add crates/fp-conformance/src/bin/coverage.rs or extend fp-ci-gates to emit COVERAGE.md from a single source of truth (scan fixtures/packets/, group by operation category, count per category, count by requirement level once that tagging lands — see sibling MEDIUM bead).
2. Wire the generator into the CI job that already runs fp-ci-gates (ci.yml 'CI gate topology' step). Fail the build if COVERAGE.md is stale.
3. Backfill the 435-fixture delta in the current doc so main is honest about coverage today.

Priority MEDIUM — doc-credibility issue, not a correctness bug. Numbers under-promise current reality, so no shipped claim is falsely elevated, but the README pulls from COVERAGE.md's narrative so the under-claim compounds upstream.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"cc-pandas","created_at":"2026-04-22T18:46:34.136345429Z","created_by":"cc-pandas","updated_at":"2026-04-23T17:45:32.944177Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T17:45:32.944177Z","close_reason":"Superseded by COVERAGE_MATRIX.md auto-generator (br-zk1j). COVERAGE.md carries a deprecation pointer + frozen-snapshot banner.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","coverage","docs","review"]}

@@ -77,6 +77,17 @@
 - **Tests affected:** `dataframe_groupby_apply`, `dataframe_groupby_apply_scalar_returns_series_indexed_by_keys`, `dataframe_groupby_apply_series_unions_sparse_result_columns`, `dataframe_groupby_apply_series_stacked_preserves_variable_labels`.
 - **Review date:** 2026-04-25
 
+### DISC-011: Int64 columns receiving null values promote to Float64 (no nullable Int64 extension dtype)
+- **Reference:** Pandas (since v0.24) has a nullable `Int64` extension dtype (capital I) that preserves the integer encoding via a separate validity mask. When a non-nullable `int64` column receives a null (e.g. via index alignment introducing rows with no source data, or via `concat(axis=1)` aligning over a non-matching index), pandas can either preserve `Int64` (extension) or promote to `float64` depending on dtype. The conformance oracle uses extension `Int64` where the column was originally `int64`.
+- **Our impl:** No nullable extension Int64 dtype yet. Int64 columns that gain null values are promoted to `Float64` with `NaN`. Downstream IO (JSON, CSV) then serializes the integer values with a trailing `.0` (`1.0` rather than `1`).
+- **Impact:** Several conformance packets exhibit `actual=Float64(1.0), expected=Int64(1)` mismatches:
+  - `FP-P2D-028` (dataframe_concat_axis1): 5 of 10 cases fail because alignment over a wider index introduces nulls into formerly-Int64 columns.
+  - `FP-P2D-433` (dataframe_to_json_records): JSON output writes `"a":1.0` instead of `"a":1` for integer columns that were promoted via null introduction.
+  - Plus other downstream packets where alignment + nulls hit Int64 columns.
+- **Resolution:** WILL-FIX - implementing nullable extension Int64 is a significant architectural change touching storage (fp-columnar), arithmetic kernels (fp-frame), and serialization (fp-io). Tracked under a future epic, not in scope for the fd90 SQL backend work. Per br-frankenpandas-mywg (fd90.76).
+- **Tests affected:** `packet_filter_runs_dataframe_concat_axis1_packet`, `packet_filter_runs_dataframe_to_json_records_packet`, plus downstream packets that hit the same root cause.
+- **Review date:** 2026-04-26
+
 ## Resolved Divergences
 
 ### DISC-005: Mixed string/numeric constructors now preserve pandas object semantics
